about.tagline: Lightweight web interface for the Raspberry Pi camera module
about.description: Utilizing the picamera2 library. This project provides a simple user interface to configure camera settings, capture photos, and manage images in a basic gallery.
about.feature_shoot_body: Take photos and video recordings with a single click and save them to the media gallery.
about.feature_configure_body: Easily configure camera settings such as image rotation, exposure, white balance settings, and many more.
about.feature_save_body: When you are happy with the configuration of your camera you can save the settings for later, even after rebooting.
about.feature_gallery_body: View, delete, and download your images and video recordings in a simple gallery interface.
about.picamera2_desc: This project utilizes the Picamera2 library for Python. Picamera2 is the libcamera-based replacement for Picamera which was a Python interface to the Raspberry Pi's legacy camera stack.
about.flask_desc: Flask is a micro web framework written in Python. It is classified as a microframework because it does not require particular tools or libraries. It has no database abstraction layer, form validation, or any other components where pre-existing third-party libraries provide common functions.
about.bootstrap_desc: Powerful, extensible, and feature-packed frontend toolkit. Build and customize with Sass, utilize prebuilt grid system and components, and bring projects to life with powerful JavaScript plugins.
about.james_desc: Raspberry Pi Enthusiast and Organiser of the Raspberry Jam Berlin and hobbyist coder.

settings.theme_desc: Sets the default theme for new or unrecognised devices. Each device can override this via the navbar toggle.
settings.lang_desc: Sets the default language for new or unrecognised devices. Each device can override this via the navbar selector.
settings.recording_max_desc: Video recording (.mp4 file) stops automatically when this duration is reached.
settings.mic_desc: Assign a microphone to each camera and configure volume and audio synchronization.
settings.mic_info_hint: Details about connected hardware (cameras and microphones) can be found in the
settings.mic_volume_tooltip: Adjusts the microphone input volume via PulseAudio. Applies immediately to the live stream. This setting is shared across all cameras using the same microphone.
settings.mic_delay_tooltip: Leave at 0 ms. Only increase this value if the audio is delayed relative to the video — i.e. you see the image before you hear the sound. An offset in the opposite direction (audio arrives before video) cannot be corrected with this setting.
settings.mic_stream_delay_tooltip: This delays the video/image in the live stream. Leave at 0 ms. Only increase this value if the audio is delayed relative to the video — i.e. you see the image before you hear the sound. An offset in the opposite direction (audio arrives before video) cannot be corrected with this setting.
settings.recording_range_error: Invalid recording duration — must be between 1 and 600 minutes.
settings.mic_stream_delay_deferred: Saved. Stream delay for {cams} will take effect after recording ends.
settings.mic_rec_delay_deferred: Recording delay for {cams} will take effect after the current recording ends.
settings.mic_delay_pending: Changes to the audio delay will take effect after the current recording has finished.
settings.liveview_page_title_desc: Identifies this device. Shown as the title above the camera feed — useful to distinguish this unit from other cameras running the same UI. Defaults to the hostname if left empty.
settings.liveview_show_title: Show as title in camera page
settings.camera_name_tooltip: This name is displayed as the camera tab label in the Live View.

hflip.name: Flip Camera Feed
hflip.info: Flip the camera feed/image horizontally (left ↔ right) or vertically (top ↔ bottom).
hflip.label: Horizontal

AfMode.name: Auto Focus Mode
AfMode.info: Controls autofocus behaviour. Manual: focus is fixed at the Lens Position set below. Continuous: the camera continuously adjusts focus automatically without user interaction.

LensPosition.name: Lens Position
LensPosition.info: Sets the lens focus distance in dioptres (1 / distance in metres). 0.0 focuses at infinity; 10.0 focuses at 10 cm. Higher values focus on closer subjects.

AfRange.name: Auto Focus Range
AfRange.info: Limits the distance range searched during autofocus. Normal: the standard range (may skip very close subjects). Macro: only the closest distances. Full: the entire focus range.

AfSpeed.name: Auto Focus Speed
AfSpeed.info: Controls how quickly the autofocus algorithm moves the lens. Fast reaches focus sooner but may be slightly less precise than Normal.

AeEnable.name: Exposure- & Gain Compensation
AeEnable.info: Auto: Automatic & continuous exposure and gain control (AEC/AGC — Auto Exposure Control / Auto Gain Control). Manual: Static and manually definable values for exposure time and analogue gain.

ExposureTime.name: Exposure Time
ExposureTime.info: Exposure time for the sensor to use, measured in microseconds.

AnalogueGain.name: Analogue Gain
AnalogueGain.info: Amplification of the light signal directly at the image sensor, before digitisation. Similar to ISO on a DSLR camera: higher values brighten the image but increase noise. A value of 1 means no amplification. Note: The smallest supported magnification factor is determined automatically and depends on the used camera (sensor). This value may be greater than 1.

ExposureValue.name: Exposure Compensation
ExposureValue.info: Exposure compensation in EV steps (each step doubles or halves the brightness). Positive values make the image brighter, negative values darker. 0.0 is the default exposure.

AeConstraintMode.name: AE Constraint Mode
AeConstraintMode.info: Constrains how the auto exposure and gain algorithm (AEC/AGC) handles different brightness regions. Normal: no special constraint. Highlight: protects bright highlights from clipping, which may result in a darker overall image.

AeExposureMode.name: AE Exposure Mode
AeExposureMode.info: Determines the range of exposure times the auto exposure and gain algorithm (AEC/AGC) may choose from. Normal: balanced range. Short: favours short exposures (reduces motion blur, may increase gain/noise). Long: allows longer exposures (better in low light, increased motion blur risk).

AeMeteringMode.name: AE Metering Mode
AeMeteringMode.info: Controls which part of the frame the auto exposure and gain algorithm (AEC/AGC) evaluates to set exposure. Centre Weighted: more emphasis on the centre. Spot: only the very centre. Matrix: the whole frame is measured equally.

AeFlickerMode.name: AE Flicker Mode
AeFlickerMode.info: When enabled, the auto exposure and gain algorithm (AEC/AGC) avoids shutter speeds that cause banding under artificial lighting. Set the Flicker Period to match your mains frequency: 10000 µs for 50 Hz power grids (100 Hz flicker), 8333 µs for 60 Hz power grids (120 Hz flicker).

AeFlickerPeriod.name: AE Flicker Period
AeFlickerPeriod.info: The period of the lighting cycle in microseconds. For example, for 50 Hz mains lighting the flicker occurs at 100 Hz, so the period would be 10000 microseconds.

AwbEnable.name: White Balance Mode
AwbEnable.info: Auto: Colour gains are continuously adjusted by the auto white balance algorithm (AWB). Manual: White balance is set manually via Colour Temperature.

ColourTemperature.name: Colour Temperature
ColourTemperature.info: Sets the colour temperature of the light source in Kelvin for manual white balance. Lower values (e.g. 2700 K) produce warm, amber tones; higher values (e.g. 6500 K) produce cool, bluish tones.

Brightness.name: Brightness
Brightness.info: Adjusts the image brightness where -1.0 is very dark, 1.0 is very bright, and 0.0 is the default 'normal' brightness.

Contrast.name: Contrast
Contrast.info: Sets the contrast of the image, where 0 means no contrast, 1.0 is the default 'normal' contrast, and larger values increase the contrast proportionately.

Saturation.name: Saturation
Saturation.info: Amount of colour saturation, where 0 produces greyscale images, 1.0 represents default 'normal' saturation, and higher values produce more saturated colours.

Sharpness.name: Sharpness
Sharpness.info: Sets the image sharpness, where 0 implies no additional sharpening, 1.0 is the default 'normal' level of sharpening, and larger values apply proportionately stronger sharpening.

still_capture_resolution.name: Photo Resolution
still_capture_resolution.info: Sets the resolution used when capturing a still image, independent of the sensor mode.

saveRAW.name: Save RAW
saveRAW.info: When enabled, the camera saves an uncompressed RAW image alongside each JPG. This RAW file is stored in DNG format and preserves all original sensor data, giving more flexibility when editing photos. When downloading a photo from the media gallery, for which a RAW-File was saved, both the JPG and the DNG file will be downloaded together.

streaming_resolution.name: Live Stream Resolution
streaming_resolution.info: Resolution used for the live stream. Can differ from recording resolution.
